titleStmt.title: Cablegram from John Dillon to John P McGoorty, 20 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Dillon

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This cablegram was sent by revolutionary John Dillon shortly after the uprising. He writes in response to John P McGoorty of Chicago, who presumably wrote him earlier saying that the Chicago Irish were pleased with the insurrection. Dillon is happy to hear the positive reaction but concerned that the insurrection was more harmful to the Irish cause than helpful. He concludes by saying that the cause depends more than ever on the unity of Irish people through out the world and especially on the support of those in America.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Dillon, John
Coll: Roger T. Faherty WESTERN UNION
CABLEGRAM NEWCOMBE CARLTON. PRESIDENT
GEORGE W.E. ATKINS, VICE PRESIDENT, BELVIDERE BROOKS VICE - PRESIDENT Received at WEEKEND LETTER
A101-CH-C-82 DUBLIN MAY 20TH 1916 WLT --- JOHN P MCGOORTY
CHICAGO (MICHIGAN BOULEVARD BUILDING)
THANKS FOR CABLE AM REJOICED TO HEAR THAT IRISH IN CHICAGO ARE SATISFIED WITH
WHAT I SAID I HAVE UNSHAKENFAITH IN FUTURE OF IRISH NATION BUT THE INSURRECTION
HAS UNDOUBTEDLY INFLICTED SEVERE INJURY ON THE IRISH CAUSE AND ALL HOPES SECUR-
ING NATIONAL SELF GOVERNMENTFOR IRELAND IN THE NEAR FUTURE DEPENDS MORE THAN
EVER ON THE UNION OF THE IRISH RACE THROUGHOUT THE WORLD AND ESPECIIALLY ON THE
SUPPORT OF THE IRISH IN AMERICA
JOHN DILLON
1059 AM
